req.title: Announcement rollout — variant 5
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for org-wide announcement publishing feature.

Requirements covered:
- System-admin can publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments
- Publishing without any department selection shall be rejected
- The action requires two-factor authentication (2FA) confirmation
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for real application objects)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class UserRole(str, Enum):
    SYSTEM_ADMIN = "system_admin"
    MANAGER = "manager"
    EMPLOYEE = "employee"


class AnnouncementStatus(str, Enum):
    DRAFT = "draft"
    PUBLISHED = "published"
    REJECTED = "rejected"


@dataclass
class User:
    user_id: str
    role: UserRole
    is_2fa_verified: bool = False


@dataclass
class Department:
    dept_id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Announcement:
    title: str
    body: str
    author_id: str
    target_departments: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    status: AnnouncementStatus = AnnouncementStatus.DRAFT


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Custom exceptions (stand-ins for real application exceptions)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PermissionDeniedError(Exception):
    """Raised when the acting user lacks the required role."""


class NoDepartmentSelectedError(ValueError):
    """Raised when an announcement is published without department selection."""


class TwoFactorAuthRequiredError(Exception):
    """Raised when 2FA has not been confirmed before a sensitive action."""


class AnnouncementNotFoundError(Exception):
    """Raised when the referenced announcement does not exist."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test (stand-in implementation that satisfies the contract)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AnnouncementService:
    """
    Thin service layer whose *interface* is what we are testing.
    In a real project this would delegate to repositories / message buses.
    """

    def __init__(self, user_repo, announcement_repo, notification_service, auth_service):
        self._user_repo = user_repo
        self._announcement_repo = announcement_repo
        self._notification_service = notification_service
        self._auth_service = auth_service

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Public API
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def publish_announcement(
        self,
        *,
        acting_user_id: str,
        announcement_id: str,
        department_ids: List[str],
        two_factor_token: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Announcement:
        """
        Publish an existing draft announcement to one or more departments.

        Raises
        ------
        PermissionDeniedError       – actor is not a system-admin
        TwoFactorAuthRequiredError  – 2FA token is missing or invalid
        NoDepartmentSelectedError   – department_ids list is empty
        AnnouncementNotFoundError   – announcement_id does not exist
        """
        actor: User = self._user_repo.get_by_id(acting_user_id)

        # 1. Role check
        if actor.role != UserRole.SYSTEM_ADMIN:
            raise PermissionDeniedError(
                f"User {acting_user_id} does not have system-admin privileges."
            )

        # 2. 2FA check
        if not self._auth_service.verify_2fa(acting_user_id, two_factor_token):
            raise TwoFactorAuthRequiredError(
                "Two-factor authentication is required to publish an announcement."
            )

        # 3. Department selection check
        if not department_ids:
            raise NoDepartmentSelectedError(
                "At least one department must be selected before publishing."
            )

        # 4. Fetch and update announcement
        announcement: Announcement = self._announcement_repo.get_by_id(announcement_id)
        announcement.target_departments = list(department_ids)
        announcement.status = AnnouncementStatus.PUBLISHED

        self._announcement_repo.save(announcement)

        # 5. Dispatch notifications
        self._notification_service.notify_departments(
            announcement=announcement,
            department_ids=department_ids,
        )

        return announcement


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def system_admin() -> User:
    return User(user_id="admin-001", role=UserRole.SYSTEM_ADMIN, is_2fa_verified=True)


@pytest.fixture()
def manager_user() -> User:
    return User(user_id="mgr-001", role=UserRole.MANAGER)


@pytest.fixture()
def employee_user() -> User:
    return User(user_id="emp-001", role=UserRole.EMPLOYEE)


@pytest.fixture()
def draft_announcement() -> Announcement:
    return Announcement(
        title="Q3 All-Hands Meeting",
        body="Please join us for the Q3 all-hands meeting on Friday.",
        author_id="admin-001",
    )


@pytest.fixture()
def departments() -> List[Department]:
    return [
        Department(dept_id="dept-eng", name="Engineering"),
        Department(dept_id="dept-hr", name="Human Resources"),
        Department(dept_id="dept-fin", name="Finance"),
    ]


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_user_repo(system_admin, manager_user, employee_user):
    repo = MagicMock()

    user_map = {
        system_admin.user_id: system_admin,
        manager_user.user_id: manager_user,
        employee_user.user_id: employee_user,
    }

    repo.get_by_id.side_effect = lambda uid: user_map[uid]
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_announcement_repo(draft_announcement):
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.get_by_id.return_value = draft_announcement
    repo.save.return_value = None
    return repo


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_notification_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture()
def mock_auth_service():
    """Auth service that approves token '123456' and rejects everything else."""
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.verify_2fa.side_effect = lambda user_id, token: token == "123456"
    return svc


@pytest.fixture()
def announcement_service(
    mock_user_repo,
    mock_announcement_repo,
    mock_notification_service,
    mock_auth_service,
):
    return AnnouncementService(
        user_repo=mock_user_repo,
        announcement_repo=mock_announcement_repo,
        notification_service=mock_notification_service,
        auth_service=mock_auth_service,
    )


VALID_TOKEN = "123456"
INVALID_TOKEN = "000000"
ANNOUNCEMENT_ID = "ann-001"


# ===========================================================================
# TEST SUITE
# ===========================================================================


class TestPublishAnnouncementHappyPath:
    """System-admin successfully publishes an announcement to departments."""

    def test_returns_published_announcement(
        self, announcement_service, draft_announcement
    ):
        result = announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=["dept-eng", "dept-hr"],
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        assert result.status == AnnouncementStatus.PUBLISHED

    def test_announcement_has_correct_target_departments(
        self, announcement_service
    ):
        target_depts = ["dept-eng", "dept-hr"]

        result = announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=target_depts,
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        assert result.target_departments == target_depts

    def test_announcement_is_persisted(
        self, announcement_service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=["dept-eng"],
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        mock_announcement_repo.save.assert_called_once()

    def test_notification_sent_to_all_selected_departments(
        self, announcement_service, mock_notification_service
    ):
        target_depts = ["dept-eng", "dept-hr", "dept-fin"]

        announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=target_depts,
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        mock_notification_service.notify_departments.assert_called_once()
        _, kwargs = mock_notification_service.notify_departments.call_args
        assert set(kwargs["department_ids"]) == set(target_depts)

    def test_publishes_to_single_department(self, announcement_service):
        result = announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=["dept-fin"],
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        assert result.status == AnnouncementStatus.PUBLISHED
        assert result.target_departments == ["dept-fin"]

    def test_2fa_service_is_called_with_correct_arguments(
        self, announcement_service, mock_auth_service
    ):
        announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=["dept-eng"],
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        mock_auth_service.verify_2fa.assert_called_once_with("admin-001", VALID_TOKEN)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestPublishAnnouncementRoleEnforcement:
    """Only system-admins may publish announcements."""

    def test_manager_cannot_publish(self, announcement_service):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="mgr-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_employee_cannot_publish(self, announcement_service):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="emp-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_permission_denied_error_message_contains_user_id(
        self, announcement_service
    ):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError, match="mgr-001"):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="mgr-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_role_check_happens_before_2fa_check(
        self, announcement_service, mock_auth_service
    ):
        """2FA must NOT be verified if role check already fails."""
        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="emp-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

        mock_auth_service.verify_2fa.assert_not_called()

    def test_role_check_happens_before_department_validation(
        self, announcement_service, mock_auth_service
    ):
        """NoDepartmentSelectedError must NOT surface if role check already fails."""
        with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="emp-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=[],          # also invalid
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestPublishAnnouncementTwoFactorAuth:
    """Two-factor authentication must be confirmed before publishing."""

    def test_missing_2fa_token_raises_error(self, announcement_service):
        with pytest.raises(TwoFactorAuthRequiredError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=None,
            )

    def test_invalid_2fa_token_raises_error(self, announcement_service):
        with pytest.raises(TwoFactorAuthRequiredError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=INVALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_empty_string_2fa_token_raises_error(self, announcement_service):
        with pytest.raises(TwoFactorAuthRequiredError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token="",
            )

    def test_2fa_error_message_is_descriptive(self, announcement_service):
        with pytest.raises(TwoFactorAuthRequiredError, match="[Tt]wo-factor"):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=None,
            )

    def test_announcement_not_saved_when_2fa_fails(
        self, announcement_service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        with pytest.raises(TwoFactorAuthRequiredError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=INVALID_TOKEN,
            )

        mock_announcement_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_notification_not_sent_when_2fa_fails(
        self, announcement_service, mock_notification_service
    ):
        with pytest.raises(TwoFactorAuthRequiredError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=INVALID_TOKEN,
            )

        mock_notification_service.notify_departments.assert_not_called()

    def test_2fa_check_happens_before_department_validation(
        self, announcement_service
    ):
        """TwoFactorAuthRequiredError must surface before NoDepartmentSelectedError."""
        with pytest.raises(TwoFactorAuthRequiredError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=[],          # also invalid
                two_factor_token=INVALID_TOKEN,
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestPublishAnnouncementDepartmentValidation:
    """Publishing without a department selection must be rejected."""

    def test_empty_department_list_raises_error(self, announcement_service):
        with pytest.raises(NoDepartmentSelectedError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=[],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_none_department_list_raises_error(
        self, announcement_service, mock_user_repo, mock_auth_service
    ):
        """Passing None instead of a list should also be rejected."""
        with pytest.raises((NoDepartmentSelectedError, TypeError)):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=None,        # type: ignore[arg-type]
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_no_department_error_message_is_descriptive(
        self, announcement_service
    ):
        with pytest.raises(NoDepartmentSelectedError, match="[Dd]epartment"):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=[],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_announcement_not_saved_when_no_department_selected(
        self, announcement_service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        with pytest.raises(NoDepartmentSelectedError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=[],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

        mock_announcement_repo.save.assert_not_called()

    def test_notification_not_sent_when_no_department_selected(
        self, announcement_service, mock_notification_service
    ):
        with pytest.raises(NoDepartmentSelectedError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
                department_ids=[],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

        mock_notification_service.notify_departments.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("dept_ids", [
        ["dept-eng"],
        ["dept-eng", "dept-hr"],
        ["dept-eng", "dept-hr", "dept-fin"],
    ])
    def test_valid_department_selections_are_accepted(
        self, announcement_service, dept_ids
    ):
        result = announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=dept_ids,
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        assert result.status == AnnouncementStatus.PUBLISHED


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestPublishAnnouncementOperationOrder:
    """Verify the security-sensitive ordering of checks."""

    def test_full_happy_path_calls_in_correct_order(
        self,
        announcement_service,
        mock_auth_service,
        mock_announcement_repo,
        mock_notification_service,
    ):
        """
        Verifies that for a valid request the sequence is:
        role-check → 2FA → dept-check → save → notify
        """
        manager = MagicMock()
        manager.attach_mock(mock_auth_service.verify_2fa, "verify_2fa")
        manager.attach_mock(mock_announcement_repo.save, "save")
        manager.attach_mock(
            mock_notification_service.notify_departments, "notify_departments"
        )

        announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=["dept-eng"],
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        call_names = [c[0] for c in manager.mock_calls]
        assert call_names.index("verify_2fa") < call_names.index("save")
        assert call_names.index("save") < call_names.index("notify_departments")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


class TestPublishAnnouncementEdgeCases:
    """Boundary and edge-case scenarios."""

    def test_duplicate_department_ids_are_handled(self, announcement_service):
        """Duplicate dept IDs should not cause a crash; deduplication is acceptable."""
        result = announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=["dept-eng", "dept-eng", "dept-eng"],
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        assert result.status == AnnouncementStatus.PUBLISHED

    def test_announcement_not_found_raises_error(
        self, announcement_service, mock_announcement_repo
    ):
        mock_announcement_repo.get_by_id.side_effect = AnnouncementNotFoundError(
            "Announcement not found."
        )

        with pytest.raises(AnnouncementNotFoundError):
            announcement_service.publish_announcement(
                acting_user_id="admin-001",
                announcement_id="nonexistent-id",
                department_ids=["dept-eng"],
                two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
            )

    def test_notification_service_receives_announcement_object(
        self, announcement_service, mock_notification_service
    ):
        announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=["dept-eng"],
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        _, kwargs = mock_notification_service.notify_departments.call_args
        assert isinstance(kwargs["announcement"], Announcement)

    def test_large_number_of_departments(self, announcement_service):
        large_dept_list = [f"dept-{i:04d}" for i in range(500)]

        result = announcement_service.publish_announcement(
            acting_user_id="admin-001",
            announcement_id=ANNOUNCEMENT_ID,
            department_ids=large_dept_list,
            two_factor_token=VALID_TOKEN,
        )

        assert result.status == AnnouncementStatus.PUBLISHED
        assert len(result.target_departments) == 500
```

---

## Design decisions & notes

| Area | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Isolation** | All external collaborators (`user_repo`, `announcement_repo`, `notification_service`, `auth_service`) are mocked so tests run without infrastructure. |
| **Check ordering** | Dedicated tests (`TestPublishAnnouncementOperationOrder`) assert the security-sensitive sequence: role → 2FA → departments → persist → notify. |
| **Negative-path completeness** | Every failure mode (wrong role, missing/invalid token, empty department list) also asserts that *no side-effects* (save, notify) occurred. |
| **Parametrisation** | `@pytest.mark.parametrize` covers one, two, and three department selections to avoid copy-paste tests. |
| **Edge cases** | Duplicate dept IDs, `None` dept list, missing announcement, and large dept lists are each covered. |
| **Error messages** | Tests verify error messages contain meaningful keywords (e.g. `"department"`, `"Two-factor"`) so failures are self-documenting. |

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for org-wide announcement publishing feature.

Requirements covered:
- System-admin can publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments
- Publishing without any department selection shall be rejected
- The action requires two-factor authentication (2FA) confirmation
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import List, Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for real application objects)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class UserRole(str, Enum):
    SYSTEM_ADMIN = "system_admin"
    MANAGER = "manager"
    EMPLOYEE = "employee"


class AnnouncementStatus(str, Enum):
    DRAFT = "draft"
    PUBLISHED = "published"
    REJECTED = "rejected"


@dataclass
class User:
    user_id: str
    role: UserRole
    is_2fa_verified: bool = False


@dataclass
class Department:
    dept_id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Announcement:
    title: str
    body: str
    author_id: str
    target_departments: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    status: AnnouncementStatus = AnnouncementStatus.DRAFT


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Custom exceptions (stand-ins for real application exceptions)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class PermissionDeniedError(Exception):
    """Raised when the acting user lacks the required role."""


class NoDepartmentSelectedError(ValueError):
    """Raised when an announcement is published without department selection."""


class TwoFactorAuthRequiredError(Exception):
    """Raised when 2FA has not been confirmed before a sensitive action."""


class AnnouncementNotFoundError(Exception):
    """Raised when the referenced announcement does not exist."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test (stand-in implementation that satisfies the contract)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class AnnouncementService:
    """
    Thin service layer whose *interface* is what we are testing.
    In a real project this would delegate to repositories / message buses.
    """

    def __init__(self, user_repo, announcement_repo, notification_service, auth_service):
        self._user_repo = user_repo
        self._announcement_repo = announcement_repo
        self._notification_service = notification_service
        self._auth_service = auth_service

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Public API
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def publish_announcement(
        self,
        *,
        acting_user_id: str,
        announcement_id: str,
        department_ids: List[str],
        two_factor_token: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Announcement:
        """
        Publish an existing draft announcement to one or more departments.

        Raises
        ------
        PermissionDeniedError       – actor is not a system-admin
        TwoFactorAuthRequiredError  – 2FA token is missing or invalid
        NoDepartmentSelectedError   – department_ids list is empty
        AnnouncementNotFoundError   – announcement_id does not exist
        """
        actor: User = self._user_repo.get_by_id(acting_user_id)

        # 1. Role check
        if actor.role != UserRole.SYSTEM_ADMIN:
            raise PermissionDeniedError(
                f"User {acting_user_id} does not have system-admin privileges."
            )

        # 2. 2FA check
        if not self._auth_service.verify_2fa(acting_user_id, two_factor_token):
            raise TwoFactorAuthRequiredError(
                "Two-factor authentication is required to publish an announcement."
            )
